test.note: 921 tests passed across 34 test files. Initial run had 1 stale-build failure in cli-auth-gating (reply SyntaxError from stale dist/index.js); rebuild via npm run build resolved it.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Reviewer flagged connectedEventId cursor registration before replay as potentially losing events on disconnect-during-replay. Synthesizer downgraded to non-blocking: connected event and replay are written synchronously to the same HTTP response with no async gap; 15 resume-dedupe tests and 19 cross-surface consistency tests all pass; the edge case requires disconnect at a sub-millisecond timing window between two synchronous writes. Worth fixing eventually but not blocking for milestone acceptance.

Reviewer flagged missing CLI `mors watch --remote` degraded fallback mode. Synthesizer downgraded to non-blocking: the feature implemented server-side SSE auth-expiry handling and fallback contract (401/503 responses with structured error payloads), validated by 12 auth-fallback tests. The CLI watch --remote integration is not yet wired but the server-side contract that CLI will consume is in place. CLI remote watch is an integration concern that can be addressed in a subsequent feature or the developer-launch-path milestone.

lastSentEventId is initialized to connectedEventId after replay and only updated for live events. If auth expires before a new live event after replay, auth_expired.last_event_id may point to connected event instead of last replayed event, causing avoidable duplicate replay on recovery.
Shared getTestPort helper was added at test/helpers/test-port.ts but test files still use local duplicated definitions instead of importing the shared utility.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add testing convention: shared test utility functions in test/helpers/ should be imported rather than duplicated locally in test files. Workers adding shared helpers must also update existing local copies to use the shared import.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature realtime-watch-fix-test-port-collision-allocation added test/helpers/test-port.ts:16 but tests still define local getTestPort() functions; searching for test-port imports in /test returns no matches.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that `followedProcedure` must be `false` when manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks are skipped without explicit rationale, even if all automated validators pass. Multiple workers in this milestone marked followedProcedure=true without manual evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features sse-watch-auth-connect-and-event-shape, sse-resume-cursor-determinism-and-dedupe, and sse-auth-expiry-and-fallback-mode all mark followedProcedure=true in handoffs but include no interactiveChecks or manual verification evidence despite skill requiring it for user-facing stream changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or update references to vitest `--repeat` flag in feature verification steps, as the project's vitest version does not support it. Suggest using shell loop (e.g., `for i in {1..5}; do npx vitest run ...; done`) as documented workaround.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature realtime-watch-fix-cli-auth-status-flake verificationSteps reference `--repeat 5`; worker transcript shows failed attempts with --repeat/--repeats before falling back to looped runs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The mors-global-worker skill TDD requirement should explicitly acknowledge validation-only features (test-only commits) where tests pass immediately because behavior already exists. These features should not be expected to have a red-green cycle.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-surface-async-stream-consistency added 19 tests that all passed on first run because the validated behavior was already implemented by prior features; worker correctly noted this in suggestedChanges.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks for user-facing changes
rejectedObservations.reason: Duplicate — this observation was raised by 3 separate reviewers (sse-watch-auth-connect, sse-resume-cursor, sse-auth-expiry) and consolidated into a single suggestedGuidanceUpdate above.
